name: rbac
description: The runtime role + permission storage layer for the sanctum + platform_admin guards. Extends spatie/laravel-permission's tables with `application_id` + `tenant_id` columns per design.md §2 D-A2. Ships CRUD APIs for tenant admins to manage their tenant's roles + custom permissions (gated by the `custom_roles` entitlement + `custom_role_slot` quota). System roles are seeded per-business-type from `role_definitions` — a metadata layer that pins which business_types default-provision which roles, plus i18n labels + min-tier gating. Enforces `GuardMismatch` (422) on any write that would bind a sanctum permission to a platform_admin role (or vice versa) + `ApplicationMismatch` (422) on cross-Application writes. Ships the base `PermissionResolver` that composes RBAC ∪ grants − denies + delegation per design.md §2 D-A3 — every downstream permission check flows through this resolver. Owns the seed permissions catalogue that domain modules extend (users.*, tenants.*, invoices.*, athletes.*, ...) — with the `.own` / `.tenant` / `.branch` / `.team` / `.child` / `.any` scope-suffix convention resolved by `auth::ScopeSuffixResolver`. Framework primitives (`#[RequirePermission]`, `#[RequireRole]`, base guard classes) live in `backend-packages/authorization/` — THIS module owns the business data. RBAC binds polymorphically to `User` (tenant plane, sanctum guard), `PlatformUser` (platform plane, platform_admin guard), and `ServiceAccount` (machine plane, sanctum guard via a dedicated ability slice).

consumedIndirectlyBy.description: Every downstream tenant-plane + platform-plane module reaches this module through the framework attributes `#[RequirePermission]` / `#[RequireRole]` (which resolve through `PermissionResolver::resolve()`) OR the composed `Gate::allows()` bridge. Domain modules NEVER query `role_has_permissions` / `model_has_roles` directly — they authorise via the resolver. The resolver is Sanctum-abilities-cached at PAT issue-time (auth module owns the caching write; RBAC owns the read-path resolution).
modules: every base repository consulting ScopeSuffixResolver (which itself consults RBAC for the caller's permissions)

wraps.rationale: spatie/laravel-permission's `roles` + `permissions` + `role_has_permissions` + `model_has_roles` + `model_has_permissions` tables are used as-shipped — we DO NOT reimplement the pivot semantics. Migrations in this module ADD columns (`application_id`, `tenant_id`, `is_system`, `description`, `sort_order`, denormalised `application_id` + `tenant_id` on the pivots) without altering the vendor-owned columns. Our `HasRoles` trait EXTENDS Spatie\Permission\Traits\HasRoles and overrides `hasPermissionTo(...)` to route through `PermissionResolver::resolve()` so RBAC + grants + delegation compose at query-time. Overriding at the trait level (not the model level) lets every model composing the trait inherit the resolver without touching call sites.

tenant.description: Tenant-facing RBAC surface. Owner + admin can CRUD custom roles (gated by `custom_roles` entitlement + `custom_role_slot` quota). Every tenant member can read their own permissions via `GET /me/permissions`. Every mutation triggers PermissionCacheInvalidated so downstream Sanctum PATs re-warm on next request.

platform-admin.description: Platform-admin RBAC surface. Manages system roles (application_id + tenant_id nullable), role_definitions metadata (business_type_slugs, i18n labels, min_tier), cross-Application observability. Every write is audit-critical.
